rulings.Case Title: ANDREW VAIL VS. GARLOCK SEALING TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, FKA GARLOCK, et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 2, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT BEARING-BELT AND CHAIN, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORIES AND THE INFORMATION PROVIDED BY MR. ORROCK. DENIED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR THE SAME REASONS. DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350(D). =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: U.S. SMOKELESS TOBACCO BRANDS INC., A DELAWARE VS. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 2, 2008 LINE 3. PLAINTIFF U.S. SMOKELESS TOBACCO BRANDS INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required. The parties are to address the following questions: 1. Is there still a dispute about the admissibility of the Ernst & Young study? 2. Is the admissibility of that report material to finding in plaintiff's favor? 3. Explain why/why not IRC section 482 is relevant to the issues? 4. SBE states its position would be the same if "plaintiff and USTMfg were completely independent." Would SBE contend that samples provided by USTMfg to X company were subject to the tax? If so, how would you calculate the tax? 5. SBE states that "the proper measure of tax is the hypothetical price at which USTMfg would sell OTP to an independent distributor." How does one calculate the hpothetical price? If this were to be the holding, how does the taxpayer calculate the tax? How does SBE determine that it is valid? What are the consequences of such a holding for other taxpayers? 6. In the State of Washington, the tax is measured by the "wholesale sales price" meaning "the established price for which a manufacturer sells tobacco products to a distributor". Explain why/why not the Washington definition is the equivalent of the California definition of "the cost of tobacco products to the distributor"? 7. How does SBE explain the holding in U.S. Tobacco II that "it is not appropriate to measure the value of OTP ... by the price ... sold to independent distributors"? It appears that this holding is contrary to SBE's position. If not, then why/why not is the Extended List Price not the equivalent of the "hypothetical price" of a sale by USTmfg? =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS DAVID RYAN VS. GUNTHER DERTZ
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 2, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT GUNTHER DERTZ'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PURPOSE OF A COMPLAINT IS TO GIVE NOTICE OF A PARTY'S CLAIM. IT IS NOT TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THE CLAIM. =(302/PJM/VC)
